sourceMovie.title: A Haunted House
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: A Haunted House works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Fright Night
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Haunted House clicked for you, Fright Night (1985) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Haunted House?
faq.answer: Try Fright Night, We Have a Ghost, Fido—each shares DNA with A Haunted House while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Haunted House?
faq.answer: A Haunted House (2013) is best described as Comedy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Haunted House part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "A Haunted House Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
